metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1020
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The authors use shRNA to knockdown (KD) AP-2 mu2 (gene AP2M1) in cultured rat hippocampal neurons. In figure 2B they use high intensity stimulation to load the neurons with FM4-64 (which is established to enter in synaptic vesicles (SVs). They wash and then stimulate exocytosis to destain the neurons (as the SVs undergo exocytosis, the FM4-64 dye is release into the culture media and looses fluorescence). Destaining in the KD is ~30% of that in control indicating a large decrease in the pool of recycling SVs.

In figure 3 they examine the rates of endocytosis of 4 major SV proteins tagged with pHlourin. They stimulate exocytosis and then examine endocytosis of SVs post-stimulation. The rates of all 4 proteins are strongly decreased.

In figure 4 they examine the rate of endocytosis of vGlutpHlourin during intense stimulation and measure a dramatic reduction.

It is important to note, knockdown of AP-2 mu2 in this manuscript leads to loss of AP-2 alpha. Thus, it is not possible to distinguish between the function of these two proteins as they are obligate heterodimers.. Experimental description: From materials and methods, "Hippocampal CA3-CA1 regions were dissected from 1- to 3-d-old Sprague Dawley rats..."

"shRNA and shRNA-resistant μ2 plasmids. Synthetic oligonucleotides (60-mers) containing the rat μ2 subunit cDNA target sequences (GTGGATGCCTTTCGCGTCA)".
Nucleotide BLAST of GTGGATGCCTTTCGCGTCA confirmed targeting of AP2M1
